text.title: Claude API Basics Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed when bootstrapping the Anthropic SDK. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (`note`).

antipattern.description: Concurrent subagents share one API key and trip the 50 req/min Tier-1 cap inside seconds.
antipattern.reason: No per-worker rate budget; no use of `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests` header to throttle proactively.
antipattern.note: Fix: divide tier capacity across N workers, throttle before reaching zero remaining, or upgrade tier. Read the header on every response and gate the next call.

antipattern.description: Cost dashboard shows fluctuating per-call prices that don't match the requested model.
antipattern.reason: Cost lookup uses the request model string; an alias has silently resolved to a different model snapshot.
antipattern.note: Fix: key the price table off `response.model`, not the request argument; assert that `response.model` matches the pin and alert on mismatch.

antipattern.description: Forked worker pool hangs on the first Anthropic call; no error, just silence.
antipattern.reason: Parent process imported `client = Anthropic()` at module top; forked workers inherited stale httpx connection state.
antipattern.note: Fix: construct the client inside the worker's `init` function (or use `spawn` start method); never share a client across forks.

antipattern.description: Random 529 errors crash the pipeline; retry policy explicitly excludes 529 because "it's not a rate limit".
antipattern.reason: Tenacity decorator only catches `RateLimitError`; `overloaded_error` arrives as `APIStatusError(status_code=529)` and bypasses it.
antipattern.note: Fix: add `retry_if_exception_type((RateLimitError, APIStatusError))` and either filter `status_code >= 500` or include 529 explicitly.

antipattern.description: Anthropic support cannot trace a failed call — operator has only the timestamp.
antipattern.reason: Wrapper never captured `x-request-id`; logs show only the exception text.
antipattern.note: Fix: read `x-request-id` from `response.response.headers` (or the SDK's exposed accessor) and emit it on every error log line. Persist alongside the request for replay.
